item.title: DIU, EWBI hold workshop to enhance univ teachers’ skills
item.description: Switzerland-based Educators Without Borders International and the Human Resource Development Institute of Daffodil International University have jointly organised a two-day international workshop titled Essentials of University Academics in Contemporary World: Building Teaching Capability and......

item.title: 4 collapsed Chuadanga bridges still unrepaired
item.description: FOUR bridges in Jibannagar upazila of Chuadanga, damaged by last year’s heavy rainfall and flash flood, remained unrepaired after more than eight months, leaving residents of around 30–35 villages struggling with disrupted connectivity, risky crossings and prolonged travel detours of......

item.title: Biman offers gifts, special meals to Eid passengers
item.description: Biman Bangladesh Airlines Limited has taken an initiative to distribute gifts to passengers on all international outbound flights on the occasion of the Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Govt takes all-out measures for safe cattle markets: livestock minister
item.description: Fisheries, livestock and agriculture minister Mohammed Amin Ur Rashid on Sunday said that the government had taken special security measures across the country’s cattle markets to ensure the safety of traders, farmers and buyers ahead of Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Bangladesh moves toward self-reliant energy system, says PM
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Sunday said Bangladesh is steadily moving toward clean energy, a green economy and a self-reliant energy system....

item.title: Cattle traders await city markets to gain momentum
item.description: Cattle traders at the capital’s sacrificial animal markets on Sunday said that trading activities were yet to gather pace, but they are expecting a significant rise in buyers from Sunday night and Monday morning, when the government holidays for Eid-ul-Azha begins......

item.title: Govt eyes boosting diversified exports to Saudi Arabia
item.description: Commerce, industries and textiles and jute minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir on Sunday called on businesses to submit sector-specific proposals aimed at expanding Bangladesh’s export presence in the Saudi Arabian market......

item.title: Banking hours near cattle markets extended
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has directed all scheduled commercial banks to keep branches and sub-branches located near sacrificial animal markets open until 10:00pm ahead of Eid-ul-Adha to facilitate secure and uninterrupted financial transactions during the peak Qurbani trading season......

item.title: Trade thru land ports shut for a week
item.description: Import-export activities between Bangladesh and India through Benapole will remain suspended for seven days while the Burimari port’s trade related activities will be closed for eight days on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha......

item.title: 37pc factories yet to pay Eid allowance
item.description: About 36.58 per cent or 3,745 factory units out of a total of 10,238 factories haven’t paid the festival allowances ahead of the Eid-ul-Azha, according to the Industrial Police data published on Sunday......

item.title: United Business Forum sweeps CCCI election
item.description: The United Business Forum, led by prominent business leader Mohammed Amirul Haque, swept the Chattogram Chamber of Commerce and Industry (CCCI) election held after a hiatus of 12 years, securing all posts......

item.title: BCCCI partners with Miup of Japan
item.description: The Bangladesh China Chamber of Commerce and Industry has signed a memorandum of understanding with Miup Bangladesh Company Limited to provide structured healthcare and medical support services for BCCCI members and affiliated companies in collaboration with Ship International Hospital......

item.title: BGB, BSF hold flag meet after border tensions
item.description: A flag meeting was held between the border forces of Bangladesh and India at the battalion commander level following the tension over the Indian force’s attempt to build a barbed wire fence on the zero line at the Dahagram Tin Bigha Corridor border in Lalmonirhat....

item.title: Japanese EV brand glafit to launch 800-bike pilot project in Bangladesh
item.description: Japanese electric vehicle company ‘glafit’ is set to launch a pilot project in Bangladesh with 800 electric bikes in the city, aiming to position the country as a regional hub for its innovative EV business model......

item.title: Hajj begins today
item.description: The formal rituals of the holy Hajj will begin on Monday as pilgrims start staying in Mina from the morning in line with the religious obligations of Hajj....

item.title: Nazrul’s 127th birth anniv today
item.description: The 127th anniversary of birth of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam will be celebrated today....

item.title: Eid shoppers hit by soaring sheep prices in Ivory Coast
item.description: Buyers trudged through a muddy market in Abidjan, looking for the best-priced sheep to be sacrificed for Eid ul-Azha, with traders driving hard bargains in the run-up to the Muslim festival......

item.title: Hotels strive to be found as AI models conduct travel search
item.description: With people increasingly adopting AI to help plan their vacations, hotels are working to make sure that you check them out — and check in......

item.title: NYC immigrant hubs eye FIFA bounce after crackdown woe
item.description: World Cup fever is beginning to hit New York’s immigrant communities where wariness of the Trump administration’s deportation crackdown has weighed on foot traffic......

item.title: Dhaka Bank holds annual managers’ confce
item.description: Dhaka Bank PLC has recently organised its Annual Managers’ Conference 2026 in Gazipur under theme of ‘Transforming Together,’ said a press release......

item.title: EBL Skybanking recognised for accessible initiatives
item.description: Eastern Bank PLC’s flagship mobile banking platform, EBL Skybanking, received ‘Recognition for Accessible Initiatives’ award in recognition of its contribution to building an inclusive and accessible digital banking ecosystem in Bangladesh......

item.title: US not to rush into deal with Iran: Trump
item.description: President Donald Trump said Sunday that he had told US negotiators ‘not to rush into a deal’ with Iran, amid anticipation that an agreement to end the war in the Middle East was close....

item.title: Shahjalal Islami Bank holds 25th AGM
item.description: Shahjalal Islami Bank PLC on Sunday held its 25th annual general meeting virtually, said a press release......

item.title: EU envoys visit Apex factory
item.description: A high-level delegation from the European Union recently visited Apex Footwear Limited’s factory at Shafipur in Gazipur to observe how global best practices are maintained in Bangladesh’s footwear and leather industry, said a press release......

item.title: Mr. Noodles hosts special prog for underprivileged children
item.description: Mr. Noodles organised a special event titled ‘Amazing Day’ to bring joy and happiness to underprivileged children, said a press release......

item.title: Stimulus package needs strict oversight
item.description: THE Bangladesh Bank has rolled out a stimulus package of Tk 600 billion to support the private sector and revive the economy. This is a welcome move by the government in view of the private-sector credit growth that has declined to a historic low of 4.27 per cent, reflecting weak business confidence, an energy crisis and mounting uncertainties. But the move leaves the......

item.title: Issues that authorities need to attend to around Eid
item.description: EVERY time Eid-ul-Azha comes, it comes with a few issues for the government to attend to. With Eid-ul-Azha only three days away, cattle markets started drawing buyers. The rush will intensify as Eid approaches. And, what the government needs to look into includes the management of the cattle market, their administration and the safety and security of both traders and......

item.title: Development trapped in delay
item.description: OVER the past two decades, infrastructure construction in Bangladesh has been used as a key instrument of economic transformation. Expressways, bridges, railroads, subway networks, thermal power plants and ports are celebrated examples of public works that symbolise the country’s vision. But behind these impressive pictures, another truth remains unnoticed......

item.title: Advancing low-carbon agro-processing
item.description: In a rice mill on the outskirts of Bogura, the steady hum of machinery masks a quieter transformation taking place. The paddy that enters as raw harvest leaves as polished grain, ready for markets across the country. Yet behind this familiar process lies an emerging concern: the carbon footprint embedded in every stage of agricultural processing. From drying and milling to......

item.title: Tracing origins of measles outbreak
item.description: STANLEY Gwavuya, acting representative of the United Nation’s Children Fund to Bangladesh, very recently spoke to a local Bangla newspaper on the current measles situation in Bangladesh. In the interview, he said that UNICEF had repeatedly warned the interim government about the possibility of a surge in measles-related cases and deaths if there was a......

item.title: Recreation crisis in Dhaka
item.description: DHAKA is one of the world’s fastest growing megacities. It is the industrial hub where there are many businesses, institutions, etc. A major concern for Dhaka is the over-saturation of restaurants with little to no place for recreational spaces. A 2025 study by environmentalists suggests that Dhaka provides less than 1 square metre of green space per capita, far below......

item.title: Dhaka Regency launches spectacular offers for celebrating Eid-ul-Azha
item.description: This Eid-ul-Adha, Dhaka Regency Hotel and Resort invites guests to immerse themselves in a world of luxury, gastronomy, wellness, and joyful celebrations through its grand Eid Re-treat campaign, specially designed to create unforgettable festive moments for families, couples, and loved ones......

item.title: Former AL, Juba Dal leaders on Ullapara Jamaat candidate list
item.description: The announcement of potential candidates by Jamaat-e-Islami in Sirajganj’s Ullapara upazila ahead of the upcoming local government elections has sparked widespread political discussion, particularly over the inclusion of two leaders previously associated with rival political parties....

item.title: Ideal School principal, 2 others charged with student assault
item.description: A case was filed against three people, including the acting principal of Ideal School and College, Rowshan Jahan, over an assault on a fourth-grade student by his seniors inside the classroom....

item.title: Woman raped, murdered at Aditmari
item.description: A housewife named Swapna Begum, 50, was allegedly strangled to death after being raped at Durgapur union of Aditmari upazila in Lalmonirhat....

item.title: July atrocities: ICT gets 555 complaints, 22 cases under trial
item.description: International Crimes Tribunal chief prosecutor Md Aminul Islam on Sunday said that the prosecution had so far accepted 105 complaints for investigation into crimes against humanity committed during the July-August 2024 uprising, while nearly 450 additional complaints were awaiting scrutiny....

item.title: Coast Guard arrests ‘criminal’ with arms in Noakhali
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested a criminal along with a single-barrel gun and two rocket flares in a joint operation at Hatiya in Noakhali on Sunday....

item.title: NCP leader Tarek Reza arrested in Jhenaidah clash case
item.description: THE police on Sunday evening arrested National Citizen Party central joint member secretary Tarek Reza in connection with a case filed by a Chhatra Dal leader over the recent clash in Jhenaidah involving activists of the two groups....

item.description: A passenger was killed after being struck by lightning while traveling on the roof of a train in Narsingdi on Sunday....

item.title: IEB celebrates 78th founding anniversary
item.description: The Institution of Engineers, Bangladesh on Saturday honoured engineers, who have been elected members of parliament, at the closing ceremony of its 78th founding anniversary celebrated as Engineers Day....

item.title: Youth sentenced to death for raping child in Meherpur
item.description: A MEHERPUR court on Sunday sentenced a young man to death for raping a nine-year-old girl in Gangni upazila of Meherpur in 2025....

item.description: This Eid, Renaissance Dhaka Gulshan Hotel invites guests to enjoy a season of celebration, relaxation, and delightful culinary experiences with an exciting lineup of festive offers across its signature venues......

item.description: An emotional Mohamed Salah said ‘we put the club back where it belongs’ after playing his final game for Liverpool on Sunday....

item.title: Guardiola goodbye spoiled by City loss to Villa
item.description:
Pep Guardiola bid an emotional farewell to Manchester City as a 2-1 defeat by Aston Villa on Sunday brought down the curtain on a glittering decade under the Catalan.

item.title: Arteta savours Arsenal's 'beautiful' trophy celebration
item.description: Mikel Arteta praised Arsenal's ‘commitment and courage’ after the Premier League champions' ‘beautiful’ trophy celebration following Sunday's 2-1 win at Crystal Palace. ...

item.description: Tottenham secured Premier League survival on a dramatic final day of the season on Sunday as West Ham's 14-year stay in the top division came to an end....

item.title: One arrested over child rape in Gazipur
item.description: The police on early Sunday arrested a man accused of raping his nine-year-old niece and recording indecent images in Gazipur....

item.title: Russia kills 4 in massive Ukraine attack
item.description: Russia pounded Kyiv with a massive bombardment that killed four people, authorities said Sunday, with Moscow unleashing its nuclear-capable hypersonic Oreshnik missile in one of the largest barrages in the more-than-four-year-long war....

item.title: Foreign loan disbursement, payment almost unchanged
item.description: The disbursement of and interest payments on foreign loans remained almost static in the 10 months (July-April) of the outgoing 2025–26 financial year....

item.title: 16 die of heatstroke in India
item.description: At least 16 people have died of heatstroke in southern India so far this summer, officials said Sunday, as a heatwave grips swathes of the country following official health warnings....

item.title: West Bengal reduces Eid holiday
item.description: The West Bengal government has declared May 28 as a public holiday on account of Eid-ul-Azha, revising its earlier holiday schedule for the festival, reports The Times of India....

item.title: Train blast claims 24 in Balochistan
item.description: A blast targeting a train carrying military personnel killed at least 24 people on Sunday in Pakistan’s turbulent southwestern province of Balochistan, a senior official said....

item.title: Govt grants pre-Eid release to 4 prisoners
item.description: The government has ordered the release of four convicted prisoners from different jails after remitting their sentences on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Info without data just opinion: minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Sunday said in the modern era, any information without data is merely a personal opinion or idea....

item.title: ADB to give $1.4b to Bangladesh for four projects
item.description: The Asian Development Bank will provide Bangladesh with $1.4 billion in financing and assistance for four major projects aimed at strengthening economic management, social protection and infrastructure development in the country....
